title: Self-Hosted Server Startup Flow
activity_flow: Load environment: try `import('dotenv/config')` — catch & ignore if not installed (optional dep)
Polyfill: if `import.meta.filename` is undefined, define it from `import.meta.url`
Dynamic import `@heyputer/backend` → destructure { Kernel, EssentialModules, DatabaseModule, SelfHostedModule, BroadcastModule, TestDriversModule, TestConfigModule, InternetModule }
Loop: for each module in EssentialModules → `k.add_module(new mod())`
branching_logic: dotenv import wrapped in try/catch — failure is silently ignored (optional dependency)
import.meta.filename polyfill — conditional: only applied if `import.meta.filename` is falsy (older Node.js versions)
EssentialModules loaded via loop — each element in the array is instantiated and registered
key_relationships: All other modules (Kernel, EssentialModules, DatabaseModule, SelfHostedModule, BroadcastModule, TestDriversModule, TestConfigModule, InternetModule) imported dynamically from @heyputer/backend default export
Kernel aggregates all modules via add_module()
Kernel.boot() triggers the full startup sequence after all modules are registered
important_notes: Top-level await used (ES module). Entry point is an IIFE: `(async () => { await main(); })()`. EssentialModules is an array of module classes looped over. LocalDiskStorageModule is the storage adapter for self-hosted (local disk). Self-hosted specific module is SelfHostedModule. TestDriversModule and TestConfigModule suggest test/dev features are included in the self-hosted build.
